body.thing: escalation handoff does not leak the weak model's reasoning
body.intention: The prior-attempt handoff dispatched at hop 1 carries the failed attempt's CONCLUSION, explicitly marked failed, and never its <think> scratchpad.

tests/inference/test_escalation_handoff.py:102: AssertionError

body.why: Measured live with one variable isolated: deepseek-r1:32b answers b4-boxes correctly on its own, adopts deepseek-r1:14b's wrong answer when handed its unmarked scratchpad, and recovers when handed only the conclusion marked failed. Escalation was transmitting the confabulation upward, on the shared path every domain uses.

stale_flags.reason: This proof asserted only that the <think> scratchpad was stripped from the handoff. That invariant is TRUE but insufficient: deepseek-r1 restates its derivation in prose OUTSIDE the think block, the stronger rung followed it, and reproduced the weak model's wrong answer on the live rack. The shipped invariant is stronger — only the failed CLAIM crosses a rung, never the argument for it. Annotated, not deleted (CP2).
